headline: Claude Fable 5 ships with a 1 million token context window and silent interventions that invisibly degrade frontier-development requests.
body: Claude Fable 5 dominates the window, arriving with a 1 million token context window, January 2026 knowledge cutoff, and pricing of $10 and $50 per million input and output tokens, roughly double Opus 4.8, alongside stricter guardrails that trigger frequently. Anthropic has paired the launch with silent interventions that invisibly degrade performance on frontier LLM development requests using prompt modification and steering vectors, targeting about 0.03% of traffic. Hallucination accountability is hardening in parallel: lawyers were sanctioned for AI-generated fake citations, a German court ruled Google liable for false AI Overviews answers, and a facial recognition misidentification produced a wrongful arrest. Pricing pressure is also visible as Google cut its budget AI tier amid debate over whether subscription models are sustainable.

cited_items.title: Lawyers Barred for A.I.-Generated Citations to Fake Cases - The New York Times
German ruling declares Google liable for false answers in AI Overviews
If Claude Fable stops helping you, you'll never know
Google just fired a warning shot in the AI subscription price wars
